private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Epic Aerospace, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Ignacio Belieres Montero, is evaluated against a peer universe of four same-sector companies at Series A and Series B stages. The comparable set spans private frontier-tech operators including ClearSpace ($400M), Hubble Network ($400M), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given the absence of disclosed revenue. The four comparables yield a median valuation of $850M, with a range from $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as target revenue is not on file—typical for private companies at this stage. The Space & Aerospace public sector trades at a median P/S multiple of 64.2x across five tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), but lacks actionable application without Epic Aerospace's revenue figure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $722M to $977M, centered on the $850M peer median. Material uncertainties include Epic Aerospace's actual revenue trajectory relative to staged peers, capital efficiency versus comparables, and the timing and magnitude of near-term financing events that would anchor market validation. The single-method reliance reflects data constraints inherent to early-stage private valuations in frontier sectors.
